title: Dialog
description: Dialog component is a flexible UI element for displaying information, prompts, or actions to users in a focused way. It offers various types, colors, and orientations for customization, and can be configured as draggable or fixed. With features like overlay control, action buttons, and adaptive sizing, the Dialog provides a seamless, accessible experience for user interactions in different contexts.

sections.title: Confirm Default Action
sections.description: Demonstrates setting the default focused action for confirm dialogs (confirm or cancel).

sections.title: Custom Buttons
sections.description: Shows how to define custom buttons with names, looks, colors, loading and click handlers.

sections.title: Programmatic Open
sections.description: Open and control dialogs programmatically using the service and AXDialogRef.

sections.title: Usage
sections.description: A simple example of using Dialog

props.name: id
props.description: Unique identifier for the component. Inherited from MXBaseComponent.

events.name: onOptionChanged
events.description: Fired when an option related to the component changes. Inherited from MXBaseComponent.

methods.content:
/**
 * Gets the host HTML element of the component.
 *
 * @returns T - The native DOM element associated with this component.
 */
getHostElement<T = HTMLElement>(): T

/**
 * Sets a component option with optional lifecycle callbacks.
 *
 * @param option - The option configuration containing the name, value and optional before/after callbacks.
 */
setOption<T = any>(option: MXComponentSetOption<T>): void

/**
 * Closes the dialog.
 *
 * @returns void - No return value. The dialog is closed and removed from the DOM.
 */
close(): void

/**
 * Show an alert box with 'Okay' button.
 *
 * @param title - The title of the alert dialog.
 * @param content - The content message of the alert dialog.
 * @param type - The type/color of the alert (primary, success, warning, danger).
 * @param orientation - The orientation of the dialog (horizontal or vertical).
 * @param draggable - Whether the dialog can be dragged.
 * @returns Promise<void> - Promise that resolves when the dialog is closed.
 */
alert(title: string, content: string, type: AXStyleColorType = 'primary', orientation: 'horizontal' | 'vertical' = 'horizontal', draggable?: boolean): Promise<void>

/**
 * Show a confirm box with 'Okay' & 'Cancel' buttons.
 *
 * @param title - The title of the confirm dialog.
 * @param content - The content message of the confirm dialog.
 * @param type - The type/color of the confirm dialog (primary, success, warning, danger).
 * @param orientation - The orientation of the dialog (horizontal or vertical).
 * @param draggable - Whether the dialog can be dragged.
 * @param defaultAction - The default action to focus (confirm or cancel).
 * @returns Promise<{ result: boolean }> - Promise that resolves with the user's choice.
 */
confirm(title: string, content: string, type: AXStyleColorType = 'primary', orientation: 'horizontal' | 'vertical' = 'horizontal', draggable?: boolean, defaultAction?: 'confirm' | 'cancel'): Promise<{ result: boolean }>

/**
 * Show a custom dialog box.
 *
 * @param options - Configuration options for the custom dialog.
 * @returns Promise<{ name: string }> - Promise that resolves with the button name that was clicked.
 */
show(options: AXDialogConfig): Promise<{ name: string }>

/**
 * Open a dialog with custom configuration.
 *
 * @param config - Configuration options for the dialog.
 * @returns AXDialogRef - Reference to the opened dialog for programmatic control.
 */
open(config: AXDialogConfig): AXDialogRef

props.name: title
props.description: The title displayed at the top of the dialog.

props.name: content
props.description: The main content message displayed in the dialog body.

props.name: type
props.description: The color theme type of the dialog (primary, success, warning, danger, etc.).

props.name: buttons
props.description: Array of button items to display in the dialog footer.

props.name: closeButton
props.description: Whether to show a close button in the dialog header.

props.name: orientation
props.description: The orientation of the dialog content and buttons layout.

props.name: icon
props.description: The icon to display in the dialog header, or 'none' to hide the icon.

props.name: draggable
props.description: Whether the dialog can be dragged by the user.

props.name: direction
props.description: The text direction of the dialog content (right-to-left or left-to-right).

props.name: text
props.description: The text label displayed on the button.

props.name: color
props.description: The color theme of the button (primary, success, warning, danger, etc.).

props.name: look
props.description: The visual appearance style of the button (solid, outline, twotone, etc.).

props.name: name
props.description: Optional name identifier for the button, used for identification when clicked.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Whether the button is disabled and cannot be clicked.

props.name: loading
props.description: Whether the button is in a loading state, typically showing a spinner.

props.name: hotkey
props.description: Keyboard shortcut or hotkey action that triggers the button click.

props.name: autofocus
props.description: Whether the button should automatically receive focus when the dialog opens.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Callback function executed when the button is clicked. The event object contains the button source and a handled flag.

methods.content:
/**
 * Closes the dialog programmatically.
 *
 * @returns void - No return value. The dialog is closed and removed from the DOM.
 */
close(): void
